methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Substrate is a Series A chip design company in Chips & Semiconductors, currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $100M+ in total fundraising. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 3 public semiconductor players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private-stage peers, with particular relevance from 3 same-sector, same-stage companies: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Substrate's $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $2.0B. Peer-Median analysis across the 3 identified same-stage comparables yields a median of $2.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied—while the semiconductor peer set carries a 25.3x price-to-sales median, Substrate's revenue is not disclosed, preventing meaningful multiples-based valuation.

The two applicable methods converge on a $2.0B median valuation, with a triangulated range of $1.7B (low) to $2.3B (high). Key uncertainties include positioning relative to the wide dispersion among private peers ($500M to $5.0B), the stage-progression assumption's fit to this market environment, and the target's undisclosed revenue and unit economics relative to public-sector margins.
